title: Between Two Castles of Mad King Ludwig

metadata.notes: Content overhaul — Wingspan teaching standard. Sources: official rulebook, Stonemaier FAQ, BGG forums, community strategy guides.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Room Tiles (147 total)
- Every tile is color-coded by room type, with its scoring condition printed on the tile:
  - **Living rooms** (purple) — score 1 VP per tile of a named type in the 8 surrounding spaces (max 8 VP)
  - **Sleeping rooms** (blue) — score 4 VP each if your castle has at least one of every other room type; only 1 VP each if any type is missing
  - **Food rooms** (yellow) — score 2 VP for each room of a named type in specific adjacent positions (above, below, or beside, as printed)
  - **Outdoor rooms** (green) — scan entire castle for rooms of a named type, 1 VP per match; nothing can ever be built above an outdoor room
  - **Corridor rooms** (white) — score 1 VP per tile with a matching wall hanging symbol in the 8 surrounding spaces
  - **Utility rooms** (orange) — score for connected or chained rooms of specified types with no cap; highest-ceiling tiles in the game
  - **Downstairs rooms** (black) — score 1–2 VP per room of a named type in the vertical column above them; must sit below the throne room row

#### Specialty Rooms & Throne Rooms
- Specialty rooms are shuffled into the general supply, each with unique scoring
- Three types earned only through bonuses: **Tower** (scores for rooms below it in its column), **Fountain**, and **Grand Foyer**
- **Each castle starts with 1 throne room tile** — shows 2 desired room types in specific positions, worth 2 VP per match; scores nothing on its own

#### Bonus Cards
- 20 bonus cards (28 in the Essential Edition), drawn between rounds
- Each adds an end-game scoring condition to one castle (e.g., "3 VP per set of 3 different room types")
- Assigned cards stay face-up — visible to all players

#### Other Components
- 1 player aid per player (room types and scoring summary)
- 1 score tracking pad
- 1 King token (first player and draft direction)
- 1 rulebook with illustrated placement examples

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Core Loop
subtopics.content:
#### You're Building Two Castles at Once
- You simultaneously build **two castles** — one shared with the player on your left, one with the player on your right
- Your personal score equals the **LOWER** of your two castles' scores
- Neglecting either castle drags your score down

#### How the Game Flows
- 2 rounds of tile drafting with a bonus phase between them

**Round 1 — Drafting Room Tiles:**
1. Everyone draws **9 room tiles** from the face-down supply
2. Secretly pick 2 tiles
3. All players reveal simultaneously — place 1 tile in your left castle, 1 in your right castle; discuss placement with each partner
4. Pass remaining hand to the **left**
5. Repeat for 4 total turns; discard the last remaining tile face-down

**Bonus Phase (Between Rounds):**
1. Draw bonus cards (count depends on player count)
2. Assign 1 bonus card to your left castle, 1 to your right castle; discard extras
3. Each bonus card adds an end-game scoring condition tied to that castle

**Round 2 — Draft Again:**
1. Everyone draws a fresh hand of 9 tiles
2. Same drafting process, but pass hands to the **right**
3. After 4 turns, discard the last remaining tile

#### Working With Your Partners
- Talk with each partner about tile placement every turn
- Both of you want the shared castle to score well, but each has a second castle pulling at your attention
- A partner who feels heard will invest more in your shared castle

#### Triggering Bonuses
- **3rd room of any type** in a castle — immediate bonus fires (extra tiles, specialty rooms, bonus cards, or royal attendants depending on type)
- **5th room of a type** — pick 1 specialty room (Tower, Fountain, or Grand Foyer) to place immediately
- Each threshold triggers only once per room type per castle

#### Placement Rules at a Glance
- Every tile must connect orthogonally to an existing tile, building outward from the throne room
- Castle grows on a grid — tiles go above, below, left, or right of existing tiles
- Once placed, tiles stay put
- **Sleeping rooms** (blue) go above the throne room row
- **Downstairs rooms** (black) go below the throne room row
- **Nothing** goes above outdoor rooms (green)

subtopics.title: Detailed Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Setup
1. **Place one throne room tile** between each pair of adjacent players (in a 5-player game, that's 5 castles in a circle)
2. **Shuffle all room tiles** face-down into a supply
3. **Shuffle bonus cards** into a face-down deck
4. **Give the King token** to a random starting player
5. **Hand each player** a player aid

#### Drafting Procedure
- Draw **9 tiles** regardless of player count
- Each turn: pick 2 secretly, reveal simultaneously, place 1 in each castle, pass remaining hand
- Round 1 passes **left**; Round 2 passes **right**
- After 4 turns, discard the 1 remaining tile face-down

#### Placement Rules
- Every tile must sit orthogonally adjacent to at least one existing tile
- Discuss placement with your castle partner — it's a shared decision
- **Downstairs rooms** (black) — must go below the throne room row
- **Sleeping rooms** (blue) — must go above the throne room row
- **Outdoor rooms** (green) — can go anywhere, but nothing may ever be placed above them
- Once placed, tiles cannot be moved

#### How Each Room Type Works
- **Living Rooms (Purple)** — count matching room types in 8 surrounding tiles; 1 VP per match (max 8 VP); place in the middle of your castle for maximum neighbors
- **Sleeping Rooms (Blue)** — score **4 VP each** if castle has at least one of every other room type (food, living, utility, outdoor, corridor, downstairs); drop to **1 VP each** if any type is missing; specialty rooms and throne room do NOT count toward the 6-type requirement
- **Food Rooms (Yellow)** — score 2 VP for each room of a named type in specific adjacent positions (above, below, or beside as printed); draft these after you know what's already in your castle
- **Outdoor Rooms (Green)** — scan entire castle for a named room type, 1 VP per match; position doesn't matter for scoring, but nothing can go above them; place at column tops or castle edges
- **Corridor Rooms (White)** — score 1 VP per tile with a matching wall hanging symbol in the 8 surrounding spaces; nestle among existing clusters of that symbol
- **Utility Rooms (Orange)** — score for connected or chained rooms of a named type with no cap; place early so they benefit from every tile drafted after them
- **Downstairs Rooms (Black)** — score 1–2 VP (as printed) per room of a named type in the column above them; place deep below the throne room, then build upward with matching tiles

#### Bonus Phase
- Draw bonus cards between Round 1 and Round 2
- Assign 1 card to each of your castles; discard extras
- Cards stay face-up — all players can see what each castle is chasing

#### 2-Player Variant (Ludwig)
- A dummy third castle "Ludwig" sits between the two players (3 castles total)
- You share one castle with your opponent and one with Ludwig
- The adjacent human player makes all placement decisions for the Ludwig side
- Your score is still the lower of your two castles

#### Specialty Rooms
- **Fountains** — specialty rooms, NOT outdoor rooms (no tree icon); you CAN build above them (confirmed in official FAQ)
- **Towers** — score for total rooms below them in their column; place at the top of a tall stack
- **Grand Foyers** — score as printed on their tile

subtopics.title: Scoring & End Game
subtopics.content:
#### When the Game Ends
- Game ends after Round 2 drafting is complete
- Every game plays exactly 2 rounds plus the bonus phase — no variable end trigger

#### Scoring Your Castles
- Score each castle individually:
  1. **Room tile points** — evaluate each tile's scoring condition based on position and neighbors
  2. **Bonus card points** — check each castle's bonus card(s) against final layout
  3. **Room type set bonus** — bonus VP for each complete set of all 7 room types
  4. **Throne room points** — 2 VP per matching room in the correct adjacent position
  5. **Specialty room points** — Towers, Fountains, Grand Foyers score per their printed conditions
  6. **Royal Attendant points** — 1 VP per wall hanging of the attendant's specified type anywhere in the castle

#### Your Personal Score
- You contributed to exactly 2 castles
- Your score equals the **LOWER** of your two castles' scores
- A 70/50 split = 50 points; a 60/60 split = 60 points — balance wins

#### Determining the Winner
- Highest personal score wins
- **Tiebreaker** — the player whose HIGHER castle scored more wins
- If still tied, shared victory

#### What Good Scores Look Like
- First game: 40–55 VP per castle
- Experienced play: 55–75 VP per castle
- Highly optimized: 75+ VP per castle

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### The Golden Rule: Balance Your Two Castles
- Your score is the **LOWER** of your two castles
- A lopsided 80/40 split = 40 points; a balanced 60/60 = 60 points
- Every turn, glance at both castles — if one is falling behind, that's where your next tile should go

#### Which Room Types to Prioritize
- **Utility rooms (orange)** — scale the hardest; no scoring cap means these can be your highest-scoring tiles; place early so they benefit from the entire game
- **Sleeping rooms (blue)** — all-or-nothing; 4 VP each with all 6 other room types present, only 1 VP without; that gap can swing 15+ points
- **Outdoor rooms (green)** — scan your whole castle (strong scaling), but nothing goes above them; place at column tops or perimeter
- **Downstairs rooms (black)** — score only for rooms in their vertical column; place deep below the throne room, then stack matching tiles upward

#### Chase Those 3rd-Tile Bonuses
- When the 3rd room of any type lands in a castle, a bonus fires (extra tiles, specialty rooms, bonus cards, or royal attendants)
- These bonuses are often worth more than any single tile's raw scoring
- Reaching 3 of a type should be a deliberate goal

#### Talk to Your Partners
- Tell each partner what you need for the shared castle
- If your left partner is loading sleeping rooms, let your right partner know you need food and outdoor rooms for type diversity
- The players who communicate most almost always score highest

#### When to Hate-Draft
- Sometimes the best pick is the tile that stops an opponent from completing a set or triggering a bonus
- Pay attention to what your neighbors are building, especially in later picks when hands are small

#### Common Mistakes
- **Neglecting one castle** — your weaker castle IS your score; check both every turn
- **Ignoring adjacency** — many rooms score based on neighbors; random placement wastes free points
- **Hoarding one room type** — six sleeping rooms but no food or outdoor rooms = missed set bonuses
- **Burying outdoor rooms** — nothing goes above them; placing one mid-column caps that column's growth
- **Glossing over bonus cards** — a bonus card can be worth 10+ VP; assign it to the castle that already fits, then keep feeding it
- **Fighting your partner** — a slightly suboptimal placement that keeps your partner invested beats a "perfect" placement that makes them give up

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Welcome to Your First Game
- Between you and each neighbor sits a throne room tile — the heart of a castle you'll build together
- You have two castles to care about: one left, one right
- Grab your player aid and review the room type summary

#### Round 1, Turn 1 — Your First Draft
1. Draw 9 room tiles from the supply
2. Pick 2 tiles — ideally one that works in your left castle and one for your right
3. All players reveal simultaneously
4. Place 1 tile in each castle — talk with each partner about placement
5. Reminder: downstairs rooms (black) go below the throne row, sleeping rooms (blue) go above
6. Pass remaining 7 tiles to the left

#### Round 1, Turn 2 — Reading the Hand
- Receive 7 tiles from the player on your right
- Look at what's already in your castles:
  - Got a food room (yellow) next to an empty spot? A matching food room would slot in perfectly
  - Got a utility room (orange) scoring for a specific type? Draft more of that type to grow it
- Pick 2, reveal, place, pass left

#### Round 1, Turns 3–4 — Shrinking Options
- Hand drops to 5, then 3 — fewer choices mean you'll adapt
- After Turn 4, discard the last tile
- Take stock of both castles: are they balanced? Is either missing a room type that sleeping rooms need for 4 VP?

#### Bonus Phase — Adding End-Game Goals
- Draw bonus cards and read them carefully
- Assign the best fit to each castle — these stay face-up for everyone to see
- Point out what you're chasing so your partners can build toward it too

#### Round 2 — Fresh Tiles, Reversed Direction
- Draw 9 new tiles; same drafting rhythm, but pass to the **right**
- Priorities:
  - Complete room type sets if sleeping rooms are in play
  - Build around your bonus cards
  - Hit 3rd-tile bonuses by getting a 3rd room of any type into a castle
- Keep talking with your partners — coordination matters more in Round 2

#### Scoring — The Moment of Truth
- Score each castle: room tile points + bonus card points + set bonuses + throne room matches + specialty room scores + royal attendant points
- Your personal score = the **LOWER** of your two castles
- First-game target: **50–60 per castle** is solid; **65+** is a great game
- The biggest lesson: the castle you ignored is the one that determined your score

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Castle Balance Is Everything
- Your score equals your LOWER castle — never let one fall more than 10 points behind
- A 75/55 split loses to a 65/65
- If your left castle is struggling, prioritize it even if your right castle could score more with that tile

#### Building Room Synergy Chains
- **Utility (orange) + focused type** — a utility scoring "1 VP per food room" alongside 5 food rooms = 5 VP from one tile, growing with every food room after it; place utility rooms early
- **Living (purple) + dense clusters** — pack living rooms into the castle center for maximum neighbors, not along edges where half the scoring zone is empty
- **Food (yellow) + sleeping (blue)** — food rooms push toward specific adjacencies, sleeping rooms push toward type diversity; together they guide a balanced, high-scoring castle
- **Downstairs (black) + tall columns** — a downstairs room scoring 2 VP for a type above it, with 3–4 matching rooms stacked overhead = 6–8 VP from a single tile

#### Activating Your Throne Room Early
- Each throne room scores 2 VP for matching rooms in specific adjacent positions
- Identify those conditions during setup and draft for them in Round 1
- A fully activated throne room can be worth 8+ VP — often the difference in a close game

#### Reading and Sending Draft Signals
- Your drafting choices tell your partner what you're building
- If you consistently place food rooms into a castle, your partner should recognize the food cluster and feed into it
- Fighting your partner's signals hurts both of you — lean into what your partner is doing

#### Timing Your Specialty Rooms
- **Towers** — score for every room below them in their column; wait until the column is 3–4 rooms tall, then cap it
- **Fountains** — specialty rooms, NOT outdoor rooms; they don't block building above; place where an outdoor room would be too restrictive
- **Grand Foyers** — score as printed; when the 5th-room bonus triggers, evaluate each specialty room against your castle's current state

#### Adjusting for Player Count
- **3–4 players** — fewer castles = more attention per castle; scores tend higher and more balanced; easier to plan ahead
- **5–7 players** — more draft competition; hate-drafting matters more; early selections carry more weight
- **2 players (Ludwig variant)** — invest enough in Ludwig to stay competitive, but don't over-invest at your shared castle's expense

rulings: Fountains are specialty rooms, NOT outdoor rooms — they lack the tree icon that identifies outdoor rooms.

Sleeping rooms require at least one of each of the 6 other regular room types (food, living, utility, outdoor, corridor, downstairs) to score 4 VP. Specialty rooms and the throne room do NOT count toward this requirement.
Downstairs rooms score for the vertical column directly above the downstairs room — not the entire castle.
Nothing may ever be placed above an outdoor room tile. This is a hard placement constraint.
Each player always draws 9 tiles per round regardless of player count.
When the 3rd room of any type is placed in a castle, an immediate bonus triggers. The 5th room of a type lets players choose one specialty room (Tower, Fountain, or Grand Foyer) to place.
Bonus cards are public information — all players can see which bonuses each castle has after assignment.
In the 2-player Ludwig variant, the adjacent human player makes all placement decisions for the Ludwig side.
